text: Handling deadlines effectively is a crucial skill in both professional and academic settings. Good time management helps you break large tasks into smaller, manageable steps, reducing last-minute stress. Prioritization allows you to focus on what is most important, so you can complete critical work first. At the same time, scope management means knowing when to say no to extra tasks that could delay your progress. Finally, clear communication is key: if you foresee a challenge, inform your team or supervisor early. They may adjust expectations or offer support. By combining these strategies, you can meet deadlines confidently and maintain a healthy work-life balance.
